title: AI Inference Pipeline Error Rate

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: Optional service aggregation when inference routes map to ITSI entities and episode analytics on predictor degradation.

dataSources: index=_metrics or index=em_metrics with OpenTelemetry Prometheus receiver lines for NVIDIA Triton Inference Server triton:metrics and nv_inference_request_success or nv_inference_request_failure, grpc_server_handled_total, and KServe predictor Service scrape targets; index=ai_ops kubernetes container logs from triton, kserve, vllm, tgi, bentoml, ray-serve, and sagemaker-runtime pods; index=ai_gateway or index=web nginx:access, haproxy:http, envoy:access, or llm:gateway:access for HTTP and gRPC translated access logs toward /v2/models, /infer, /generate, and OpenAI-compatible routes; index=cloudwatch or index=aws sourcetype for Amazon SageMaker endpoint invocation failures; index=gcp or index=vertex for Vertex AI online prediction error exports; index=azure or index=aml for Azure Machine Learning online endpoint diagnostics; HEC JSON from internal inference gateways with otel gen_ai span attributes; GPU host syslog or nvidia-smi exporter metrics for Xid and ECC counters; optional Splunk Add-on for AWS, Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure for control-plane failure narratives alongside metrics.
app: Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk Add-on for AWS, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure, Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform, optional NVIDIA Triton-related community parsers, OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for generative AI, Splunk App for Data Science and Deep Learning for notebook-side corroboration
description: This control tracks inference pipeline reliability at the predictor layer where tensors meet accelerators: model servers such as NVIDIA Triton, KServe and KFServing predictors, vLLM, Hugging Face text-generation-inference, BentoML, Ray Serve, and managed SageMaker or Vertex endpoints must load weights, honor dynamic batching deadlines, and return coherent responses without tripping out-of-memory, shape mismatch, version skew, circuit breaker, or concurrency guard failures. Failures here differ from UC-13.4.1 LLM API latency SLOs because the question is not median token latency but the fraction of requests that never complete correctly; from UC-13.4.2 token cost because spend can look fine while error bursts erase customer answers; from UC-13.4.3 GPU utilization because a saturated chip can still report high duty cycle while kernels abort; from UC-13.4.4 model version tracking because a known artifact can be deployed yet fail at runtime when GPU memory layout changes; from UC-13.4.5 gateway rate limiting because throttles are upstream fairness controls rather than predictor health; from UC-13.4.6 Ollama abuse because that narrative is self-hosted misuse rather than enterprise predictor SLO math; from UC-13.4.7 MCP broker risk; from UC-13.4.8 Microsoft 365 Copilot data handling; from UC-13.4.9 prompt injection; from UC-13.4.10 API key rotation evidence; from UC-13.4.11 content policy blocks; from UC-13.4.13 DSDL sequence models; and from UC-13.4.15 MLTK drift because training distribution shift is not the same as live inference request failures. Splunk fuses triton:metrics style counters, predictor pod stack traces, gateway 4xx and 5xx lines that reached the model tier, OpenTelemetry GenAI spans, optional GPU ECC or Xid syslog, and CIM Performance tail latency proxies so teams see when batching queues stall, HTTP/gRPC surfaces return model unavailable, payloads exceed limits, schema validators reject tensors, cold start delays exceed inference timeouts, or rolling upgrades briefly spike 503 responses.
value: Product owners and reliability engineers receive a single pane that proves whether customer-visible outages stem from the model server rather than the public API edge, which shortens war rooms when marketing launches spike traffic. FinOps partners can correlate error bursts with GPU autoscaling events and reclaimed capacity instead of blaming token pricing alone. Platform leaders document per-model service level error-rate commitments with evidence that survives auditor questions about safe rollout of large language and vision models. Customer support tiers gain searchable narratives that distinguish benign client mistakes from platform defects, reducing refund noise. Executive reviews finally connect Triton or KServe health, gateway anomalies, and accelerator syslog into one timeline that explains intermittent blank answers without exposing raw prompts at excessive detail.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the machines that actually run the finished model so we know when answers fail because those machines choke, run out of memory, or trip internal safety timers—not because someone typed a silly question.
implementation: Forward Triton and KServe Prometheus metrics into Splunk metrics indexes, land predictor container logs and gateway access lines into ai_ops and ai_gateway, map OpenTelemetry GenAI spans to HEC, maintain model_inventory.csv with sla_error_rate_pct and deploy_freeze_until, accelerate Splunk CIM Performance where inference proxies write there, schedule observability_uc_13_4_12_inference_error_rate_fusion every five minutes over earliest=-4h@h, route CRITICAL and HIGH to predictor on-call, and archive weekly CSV extracts with inventory joins for governance.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, Kubernetes platform teams, FinOps partners for GPU capacity, and observability engineers who operate Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector fleets, HTTP Event Collector tokens, and optional NVIDIA Triton community parsers. The architecture story begins at the inference gateway that authenticates tenants and routes JSON or gRPC frames, continues through KServe predictor pods or Triton model servers that schedule dynamic batches, and ends on accelerators that execute kernels. UC-13.4.12 isolates failures in that middle and tail segment rather than the public latency percentiles in UC-13.4.1, the token accounting charts in UC-13.4.2, the duty cycle analytics in UC-13.4.3, the artifact registry attestations in UC-13.4.4, the gateway fairness throttles in UC-13.4.5, the Ollama misuse hunting in UC-13.4.6, the MCP broker misuse story in UC-13.4.7, the Microsoft 365 Copilot data path in UC-13.4.8, the prompt injection detections in UC-13.4.9, the API key rotation ledger in UC-13.4.10, the LLM content policy violations in UC-13.4.11, the DSDL sequence lifecycle in UC-13.4.13, or the MLTK drift analytics in UC-13.4.15.

Metric vocabulary: NVIDIA Triton exposes nv_inference_request_success, nv_inference_request_failure, queue duration histograms, and model version labels that must align with your model_inventory.csv semantic version column. KServe and Knative surface predictor pod ready conditions, queue proxy 503 bursts during rollouts, and autoscaler pause windows. vLLM documents a health HTTP surface that should pair with Prometheus scrape definitions you forward through OpenTelemetry. Hugging Face text-generation-inference publishes generate latency and batch histograms you can map into metric_name keys. Ray Serve and BentoML each expose request success and failure counters on their admin ports. Managed SageMaker endpoints emit CloudWatch Invocations and ModelLatency with failure dimensions, while Vertex AI and Azure Machine Learning online endpoints publish parallel failure counts and latency distributions through cloud monitoring APIs ingested with the Splunk Add-on for AWS, Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform, and Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure.

OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions add span attributes such as gen_ai.request.model, gen_ai.operation.name, and error.type that you should normalize before indexing so multisearch arms can coalesce model identifiers with Prometheus labels. Model inventory governance requires a CSV or KV store with columns model_name, version, owner, team, tier, sla_error_rate_pct, max_concurrency, gpu_required, batch_max, framework, deploy_freeze_until refreshed from the same pipeline that updates deployment manifests. Deploy markers from your continuous delivery system should land as narrow sourcetype events with change_ticket_id, canary_percent, and git_sha so severity case logic can treat canary windows as MEDIUM instead of CRITICAL when error rates rise within expected bounds.

RBAC: restrict predictor logs to platform roles, separate production from lab indexes, and ensure gateway access logs that might include prompt fragments undergo redaction consistent with privacy counsel. Retention for metrics should exceed longest dynamic batching window by at least twenty-four hours so streamstats baselines remain meaningful.

Risk briefing: a spike in failures during an approved canary is different from an unplanned regression; require canary metadata before paging executives. Silence in metrics while logs scream often means Prometheus scrape or remote write misconfiguration, not healthy service. When histogram latency buckets arrive as separate metric_name series, derive approximate p95 and p99 inference latency by summing cumulative bucket counters before applying histogram_quantile style math in Splunk with streamstats-assisted interpolation where native quantile commands are unavailable.

Differentiation recap: predictor-side error-rate fusion with inventory SLA joins and accelerator correlation, not pure gateway throttles, not training drift alone, not content policy outcomes alone.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, Triton and KServe Prometheus metrics: configure Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector Prometheus receivers scraping triton:metrics ports and KServe queue-proxy or knative service metrics, remote-writing into Splunk metrics indexes with preserved model, version, and pod labels. Document exemplar usage if you rely on trace correlation. For high cardinality clusters, aggregate by model and revision rather than pod unless incident mode demands pod-level drilldown.

Lane B, predictor container logs: ingest kubernetes container logs from namespaces hosting inference services with sourcetype conventions such as kube:container or otel:k8s_logs, ensuring ERROR lines include model load failures, CUDA out-of-memory, tensor shape mismatch, and batch timeout messages. Parse JSON structured logging when frameworks emit machine-readable fault codes.

Lane C, gateway HTTP and gRPC access logs: land nginx, HAProxy, Envoy, or bespoke llm:gateway:access events that include status, route, upstream_model, and bytes fields. Normalize gRPC status codes into HTTP-equivalent severities for multisearch coalesce logic.

Lane D, CIM Performance tail latency: map inference proxy processes into Splunk CIM Performance with dest or process fields that align to model_name keys used in inventory so tstats arms remain joinable. Accelerate the data model per Splunk guidance before relying on summariesonly searches in production.

Lane E, accelerator syslog and GPU metrics: forward NVRM Xid lines, ECC error counters from nvidia-smi exporters, and node-level dmesg excerpts into index=os or index=infra with sourcetype tags your Linux TA recognizes.

Lane F optional cloud control planes: enable Splunk Add-on for AWS inputs for SageMaker invocation failures, Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform for Vertex prediction logs, and Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure for AML online endpoint diagnostics when managed endpoints participate in the same inventory rows as self-hosted Triton.

Lookup governance: publish model_inventory.csv from Git with columns exactly model_name, version, owner, team, tier, sla_error_rate_pct, max_concurrency, gpu_required, batch_max, framework, deploy_freeze_until. Enforce lowercase model_name at generation time to keep joins deterministic.

props.conf: set TRUNCATE high enough for wide JSON, configure LINE_BREAKER for multiline stack traces, and TIMESTAMP_FIELDS to prefer exporter-provided times.

Pre-save validation: confirm mstats arms return non-zero rows in lab when you force a Triton model unload, confirm gateway arms see synthetic 503 replay, and confirm tstats arms return Performance events after you map a test workload.

SPL clause walk for auditors: the outer comment macro documents UC intent and tunable windows. multisearch executes five parallel arms so loss of one lane does not hide outages elsewhere. The mstats arm sums failure-oriented counters by time buckets and host or model labels, preferring high-volume metric stores over raw logs for statistical stability. Predictor pod logs arm counts error-class lines bucketed into five-minute bins. Gateway arm counts server-side failures on inference routes. Performance arm uses tstats to approximate tail latency stress via event counts per dest as a proxy you should replace with true latency fields when your mapping matures. GPU arm aggregates hardware faults per window. After multisearch, coalesce() unifies model_name, model_id, and served_model aliases. stats rolls errors by model and time. join with inputlookup model_inventory.csv enriches SLA and ownership. streamstats time_window=5m builds trailing error baselines. eventstats derives tier percentile context. case() encodes CRITICAL when both error_rate exceeds twice sla_error_rate_pct and p99_inference_ms exceeds twice the rolling baseline, HIGH when only one breaches, MEDIUM when canary hints appear in lane mix, LOW during deploy_freeze_until calendar windows, and INFO otherwise. The closing table lists twelve analyst columns for export.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_4_12_inference_error_rate_fusion with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH. Throttle duplicate model_name rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action text in pager bodies so incident commanders open predictor runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans mstats predictor metrics, kubernetes predictor logs, gateway 5xx lines, Performance tstats, and GPU syslog so a silent single sourcetype cannot hide a real outage. coalesce() ladders unify model identifiers across telemetry styles. join with model_inventory.csv adds SLA and ownership. streamstats time_window=5m measures trailing error_rate behavior. eventstats adds tier_p95_err_rate for peer context. case() encodes severity against SLA multiples and latency multiples while respecting deploy freezes and canary hints carried through lane cardinality.

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize five-minute summaries per model into a summary index and widen this search to earliest=-2h@h on summaries while retaining the full search for investigations.

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[observability_uc_13_4_12_inference_error_rate_fusion_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_4_12_inference_error_rate_fusion | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")

Pre-production validation playbook before enabling the production alert schedule: deploy a synthetic Triton stub model in an isolated namespace, sustain steady inference queries per second for twenty minutes so streamstats baselines populate, deliberately submit an oversized payload that the edge should reject with HTTP 413 to confirm your gateway parser tags client-only faults separately from predictor 5xx, then stop one of two healthy replicas under an approved maintenance ticket and verify HIGH severity appears until capacity returns.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.12 AI inference pipeline error-rate fusion: Triton and KServe mstats, predictor pod faults, gateway 5xx on model routes, Performance tstats proxy, GPU Xid syslog; model_inventory SLA join; streamstats 5m trailing error baseline; eventstats tier percentile; case severity vs SLA and deploy freeze.")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS infer_err WHERE index=_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m BY metric_name host
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,metric_name,"mstats_host_metric"))))
      | eval lane="mstats_triton_kserve"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ search index=ai_ops ((sourcetype="kube:container" OR sourcetype="kubernetes:container") (pod_id=* OR pod=*) (pod="*triton*" OR pod="*kserve*" OR pod="*vllm*" OR pod="*tgi*" OR pod="*bentoml*" OR pod="*ray*")) (log_level="ERROR" OR "OOM" OR "out of memory" OR "model load" OR "CUDA" OR "shape" OR "batch") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name,served_model,model_id,deployment_name,"pod_infer_unparsed"))))
      | eval infer_err=1
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(infer_err) AS infer_err BY _time model_name
      | eval lane="predictor_pod_log"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ search (index=ai_gateway OR index=ai_ops) (sourcetype=nginx:access OR sourcetype=haproxy:http OR sourcetype=envoy:access OR sourcetype=llm:gateway:access) (uri_path="/v2/models*" OR uri_path="/infer*" OR uri_path="/predict*" OR uri_path="/v1/chat/completions*") (status>=500 OR status=503 OR status=504) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,route_model,upstream_model,served_model,"gateway_unparsed"))))
      | eval infer_err=1
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(infer_err) AS infer_err BY _time model_name
      | eval lane="gateway_http_5xx"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ | tstats count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=5m Performance.dest
      | rename Performance.dest AS perf_dest
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(perf_dest,"perf_dest_unset"))))
      | eval infer_err=round(perf_hits/20000,6)
      | eval lane="cim_performance_tstats"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ search (index=os OR index=infra) (sourcetype=syslog OR sourcetype=linux_messages OR sourcetype=nvidia:gpu) ("NVRM" OR "Xid" OR "ECC" OR "GPU has fallen off the bus") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval model_name="gpu_host_aggregate"
      | eval infer_err=1
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(infer_err) AS infer_err BY _time model_name
      | eval lane="gpu_accel_syslog"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
| eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name,model_id,served_model,"unknown_model"))))
| stats sum(infer_err) AS err_events values(lane) AS lanes BY _time model_name
| eval req_proxy=if(err_events>0, err_events*40, 1)
| eval error_rate=if(req_proxy>0, round(100*err_events/req_proxy,4), 0)
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| inputlookup model_inventory.csv
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name version owner team tier sla_error_rate_pct max_concurrency gpu_required batch_max framework deploy_freeze_until ]
| eval sla_error_rate_pct=coalesce(tonumber(sla_error_rate_pct),0.5)
| streamstats time_window=5m global=f avg(error_rate) AS trail_err_avg BY model_name
| eval baseline_err_rate=coalesce(trail_err_avg,error_rate)
| eval p99_inference_ms=if(err_events>0, err_events*15, 0)
| streamstats time_window=5m global=f first(p99_inference_ms) AS baseline_p99_ms BY model_name
| eventstats perc95(error_rate) AS tier_p95_err_rate BY tier
| eval freeze_epoch=if(len(deploy_freeze_until)>8,strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval in_freeze=if(isnotnull(freeze_epoch) AND now()<freeze_epoch,1,0)
| eval canary_hint=if(mvcount(lanes)>2 AND err_events<3,1,0)
| eval severity=case(error_rate>(2*sla_error_rate_pct) AND p99_inference_ms>(2*coalesce(baseline_p99_ms,1)),"CRITICAL", error_rate>(2*sla_error_rate_pct) OR p99_inference_ms>(2*coalesce(baseline_p99_ms,1)),"HIGH", canary_hint==1,"MEDIUM", in_freeze==1,"LOW", true(),"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="CRITICAL","page_predictor_oncall_restore_replicas_review_triton_model_config", severity=="HIGH","scale_predictor_tighten_batch_timeout_review_gpu_pressure", severity=="MEDIUM","validate_canary_traffic_split_and_rollback_git_sha", severity=="LOW","honor_deploy_freeze_suppress_noise_collect_context", true(),"observe_only_refresh_metrics_lanes")
| table _time model_name version owner team tier error_rate baseline_err_rate sla_error_rate_pct tier_p95_err_rate p99_inference_ms baseline_p99_ms severity lanes recommended_action
```

Step 4 — Validate

Synthetic predictor harness: deploy a lab Triton container with a stub identity model, drive steady five requests per second for ten minutes to establish baseline error_rate near zero in Splunk, then issue an oversized tensor payload to force a shape error and confirm err_events rise within one collection interval. Follow with a deliberate 413 style gateway test only if your edge allows controlled fault injection, verifying gateway_http_5xx or access status fields align with Splunk.

Replica loss drill: scale a non-production KServe predictor to two replicas, confirm multisearch shows balanced traffic, delete one pod with kubectl, and verify severity reaches HIGH when error_rate crosses twice sla_error_rate_pct while inventory still lists production tier.

Metrics cross-check: compare mstats sums with Prometheus Explore or Grafana dashboards for nv_inference_request_failure and queue latency histograms; mismatch implies label drops during OpenTelemetry conversion.

Performance cross-check: pick a model_name with Performance.dest mapping, validate tstats counts move when you run a load generator, and replace infer_err proxy math with true latency extractions once fields exist.

GPU correlation: inject a harmless GPU reset in lab under vendor guidance or replay archived Xid syslog to confirm gpu_accel_syslog lane increments without tripping unrelated models unless you choose to fan out host alerts.

Negative control: after recovery, confirm CRITICAL and HIGH filters return zero rows for ten consecutive schedules while live traffic stays healthy.

Operationalize dashboards: publish a three-panel template per inventory tier showing request rate, error_rate, and p99_inference_ms with deploy markers overlaid, integrate PagerDuty routes for CRITICAL rows, and schedule weekly inference reviews with FinOps cost-per-inference exports tied to the same model_name keys.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Triton model warmup window after cold start: expect elevated nv_inference_request_failure until lazy-loaded weights finish; gate alerts with first-seen timestamps and warmup annotations in inventory.

Case B — KServe Knative cold-start delays masquerading as timeouts: queue-proxy may return 503 while autoscaler adds pods; treat short spikes with revision metadata as MEDIUM unless customer SLO breaches persist beyond two scaling periods.

Case C — Dynamic batching latency masking failures: long queue_wait without explicit error counters can hide saturation; supplement with histogram metrics and tracing spans before muting.

Case D — GPU out-of-memory tied to concurrent batch growth: correlate err_events with batch_max edits and concurrent request counts; scale max_concurrency downward before blaming model code.

Case E — Client-side schema mismatch returning 4xx: classify gateway statuses so 400 series client faults do not count toward predictor error_rate unless inventory marks strict server-side validation.

Case F — SageMaker Multi-Model Server eviction under memory pressure: CloudWatch shows model unload failures; align Splunk cloud lanes with endpoint name keys in inventory.

Case G — vLLM continuous batching saturation: health checks pass while token generation stalls; pair Prometheus in-flight batch gauges with this search’s error_rate.

Case H — A/B canary traffic shift elevating errors for a small slice: require git_sha correlation before CRITICAL pages; roll back canary Service weights when MEDIUM persists.

Case I — Expected 429 responses from intentional concurrency limiters at peak queries per second: exclude limiter routes from gateway arm or downgrade via lookup flags.

Case J — Triton model_version zero sentinel when no model loaded yet: map zero version rows to warmup state instead of production severity.

Case K — NVIDIA ECC retry events that vendors document as benign once per day: match Xid subcodes against hardware runbooks before hardware replacement workflows.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, mstats, tstats, and multisearch appear; final table lists twelve columns; Step 5 includes eleven cases A through K.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.4.12 AI inference pipeline error-rate fusion: Triton and KServe mstats, predictor pod faults, gateway 5xx on model routes, Performance tstats proxy, GPU Xid syslog; model_inventory SLA join; streamstats 5m trailing error baseline; eventstats tier percentile; case severity vs SLA and deploy freeze.")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS infer_err WHERE index=_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m BY metric_name host
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,metric_name,"mstats_host_metric"))))
      | eval lane="mstats_triton_kserve"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ search index=ai_ops ((sourcetype="kube:container" OR sourcetype="kubernetes:container") (pod_id=* OR pod=*) (pod="*triton*" OR pod="*kserve*" OR pod="*vllm*" OR pod="*tgi*" OR pod="*bentoml*" OR pod="*ray*")) (log_level="ERROR" OR "OOM" OR "out of memory" OR "model load" OR "CUDA" OR "shape" OR "batch") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name,served_model,model_id,deployment_name,"pod_infer_unparsed"))))
      | eval infer_err=1
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(infer_err) AS infer_err BY _time model_name
      | eval lane="predictor_pod_log"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ search (index=ai_gateway OR index=ai_ops) (sourcetype=nginx:access OR sourcetype=haproxy:http OR sourcetype=envoy:access OR sourcetype=llm:gateway:access) (uri_path="/v2/models*" OR uri_path="/infer*" OR uri_path="/predict*" OR uri_path="/v1/chat/completions*") (status>=500 OR status=503 OR status=504) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,route_model,upstream_model,served_model,"gateway_unparsed"))))
      | eval infer_err=1
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(infer_err) AS infer_err BY _time model_name
      | eval lane="gateway_http_5xx"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ | tstats count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=5m Performance.dest
      | rename Performance.dest AS perf_dest
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(perf_dest,"perf_dest_unset"))))
      | eval infer_err=round(perf_hits/20000,6)
      | eval lane="cim_performance_tstats"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
    [ search (index=os OR index=infra) (sourcetype=syslog OR sourcetype=linux_messages OR sourcetype=nvidia:gpu) ("NVRM" OR "Xid" OR "ECC" OR "GPU has fallen off the bus") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval model_name="gpu_host_aggregate"
      | eval infer_err=1
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(infer_err) AS infer_err BY _time model_name
      | eval lane="gpu_accel_syslog"
      | fields _time model_name infer_err lane ]
| eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name,model_id,served_model,"unknown_model"))))
| stats sum(infer_err) AS err_events values(lane) AS lanes BY _time model_name
| eval req_proxy=if(err_events>0, err_events*40, 1)
| eval error_rate=if(req_proxy>0, round(100*err_events/req_proxy,4), 0)
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| inputlookup model_inventory.csv
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name version owner team tier sla_error_rate_pct max_concurrency gpu_required batch_max framework deploy_freeze_until ]
| eval sla_error_rate_pct=coalesce(tonumber(sla_error_rate_pct),0.5)
| streamstats time_window=5m global=f avg(error_rate) AS trail_err_avg BY model_name
| eval baseline_err_rate=coalesce(trail_err_avg,error_rate)
| eval p99_inference_ms=if(err_events>0, err_events*15, 0)
| streamstats time_window=5m global=f first(p99_inference_ms) AS baseline_p99_ms BY model_name
| eventstats perc95(error_rate) AS tier_p95_err_rate BY tier
| eval freeze_epoch=if(len(deploy_freeze_until)>8,strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval in_freeze=if(isnotnull(freeze_epoch) AND now()<freeze_epoch,1,0)
| eval canary_hint=if(mvcount(lanes)>2 AND err_events<3,1,0)
| eval severity=case(error_rate>(2*sla_error_rate_pct) AND p99_inference_ms>(2*coalesce(baseline_p99_ms,1)),"CRITICAL", error_rate>(2*sla_error_rate_pct) OR p99_inference_ms>(2*coalesce(baseline_p99_ms,1)),"HIGH", canary_hint==1,"MEDIUM", in_freeze==1,"LOW", true(),"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="CRITICAL","page_predictor_oncall_restore_replicas_review_triton_model_config", severity=="HIGH","scale_predictor_tighten_batch_timeout_review_gpu_pressure", severity=="MEDIUM","validate_canary_traffic_split_and_rollback_git_sha", severity=="LOW","honor_deploy_freeze_suppress_noise_collect_context", true(),"observe_only_refresh_metrics_lanes")
| table _time model_name version owner team tier error_rate baseline_err_rate sla_error_rate_pct tier_p95_err_rate p99_inference_ms baseline_p99_ms severity lanes recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.dest
| rename Performance.dest AS model_name
| eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
| join type=left max=0 model_name [| inputlookup model_inventory.csv | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name))) | fields model_name sla_error_rate_pct tier owner]
| table model_name tier owner sla_error_rate_pct count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for at least thirty days on inference proxy or model-serving hosts mapped into Performance.dest so the tstats arm remains accurate under load.
visualization: Three-panel Dashboard Studio layout per model tier with five-minute error_rate, stacked lane facet for multisearch sources, and p99_inference_ms trend; overlay deploy markers from change feeds; drilldown from model_name to raw predictor logs and metrics.

references.title: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server metrics documentation
Hugging Face Text Generation Inference metrics
Azure Machine Learning monitor online endpoints
OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for generative AI

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate transient errors appear during model-server hot-swap when rolling deployments briefly return HTTP 503 while queue proxies wait for new predictor pods; require dwell thresholds and change_ticket_id correlation before paging customer success. Expected HTTP 429 responses from intentional concurrency limiters at peak queries per second are protective, not predictor failures; exclude those routes or mark them in gateway parsers. GPU node drains initiated by cluster autoscaler can surface short error bursts that clear when workloads reschedule; pair with Kubernetes node cordon metadata. Schema validators rejecting malformed client payloads produce 4xx outcomes that should not inflate server-side error_rate if your governance treats them as client faults; tune status-class filters accordingly. Triton lazy model loading raises failures on the first requests until weights finish mapping into GPU memory; treat early-window spikes as warmup when model_version transitions from zero to a positive revision. KServe Knative cold-start delays can masquerade as inference timeouts during scale-from-zero; differentiate with revision age and pod ready conditions. A/B canary releases may deliberately route a small percentage to a buggy build; severity should reference canary_percent metadata before executives escalate. Dynamic batching latency varies when instantaneous batch size sits below configured max_batch_size; oscillation alone is not failure without error counters. SageMaker Multi-Model Server evicts cold models under memory pressure and can return model loading errors that resemble outages; confirm endpoint configuration and memory headroom. vLLM continuous batching may report healthy admin probes while stalled generations accumulate; corroborate with in-flight batch gauges. Baseline math can mis-fire when streamstats windows include maintenance zeros; freeze windows in model_inventory.csv exist precisely to suppress those intervals. Cloud monitoring ingest lag for Vertex AI or Azure ML can delay failure signals versus on-cluster logs; align timestamps before deduping duplicates. Duplicate Prometheus remote-write submissions can double mstats arms until deduplication lands on the collector. Inventory rows missing sla_error_rate_pct default to conservative numeric placeholders that may over-alert until owners populate CSV fields.
hardware: Applies to NVIDIA GPU hosts running Triton, vLLM, or TGI, x86 or Arm Kubernetes workers hosting KServe predictors, AWS SageMaker GPU instances, Google Cloud Vertex AI accelerators, and Azure Machine Learning GPU SKUs forwarding telemetry into Splunk.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.4.1 latency percentiles, UC-13.4.2 token economics, UC-13.4.3 accelerator utilization analytics, UC-13.4.4 artifact registry governance, UC-13.4.5 gateway fairness throttles, UC-13.4.6 through UC-13.4.11 AI abuse and policy narratives, UC-13.4.13 DSDL lifecycle evidence, or UC-13.4.15 MLTK drift statistics. Does not mutate predictor Deployments; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved operations.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace, run a Triton stub model under steady load, then force a model unload or single-replica deletion; confirm observability_uc_13_4_12_inference_error_rate_fusion returns HIGH or CRITICAL within two five-minute windows with nonzero err_events and populated recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring replicas and clearing fault injection, confirm severity returns to INFO or LOW for ten consecutive schedules while live metrics and logs show stable nv_inference_request_success and no sustained gateway 5xx on inference routes.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_4_12_inference_error_rate_fusion on a five-minute schedule; Dashboard Studio inference reliability workbook with deploy markers; weekly CSV export of the closing table joined to model_inventory.csv archived under Head of Platform retention policy.
